subject: English
chapter: The Tale of Custard the Dragon
title: The Tale of Custard the Dragon – Medium Short Notes

key_points: The poem 'The Tale of Custard the Dragon' is written by Ogden Nash
Belinda lives in a little white house with four pets: Ink, Blink, Mustard and Custard
All the pets claim to be very brave while Custard is mocked as cowardly
Custard constantly cries for a safe cage, which makes others laugh at him
A pirate suddenly enters the house and creates danger
Belinda and her pets become frightened and run away or hide
Custard bravely faces the pirate and defeats him
After the danger passes, the others begin boasting again
Custard remains humble and says everyone else is braver than him
characters: Belinda – A young girl who lives with several pets and is described as very brave
Ink – Belinda’s black kitten who pretends to be brave but becomes frightened during danger
Blink – A grey mouse who hides when the pirate enters
Mustard – A yellow dog described as brave as a tiger but runs away when danger appears
Custard – A dragon who appears cowardly but proves to be the bravest character
Pirate – A dangerous intruder who threatens Belinda and her pets
themes: True bravery is revealed through actions in difficult situations
People often boast about qualities they do not truly possess
Humility is an admirable trait as shown by Custard
Appearances may not reflect true character
literary_devices: Simile – Used to compare bravery such as 'as brave as a tiger in a rage'
Hyperbole – Exaggeration is used to describe the bravery of Belinda and her pets
Irony – The dragon considered cowardly turns out to be the bravest
Humorous tone – The poem uses playful language and exaggeration
exam_tips: Remember the sequence of events leading to the pirate attack
Highlight the contrast between the pets’ boasting and their actual behaviour
Explain how Custard becomes the hero of the poem
Mention simile and irony when writing about literary devices
